title: Deguelia canarensis
taxonomy: Deguelia canarensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes or beans. It belongs to the genus Deguelia, which is a group of tropical trees and shrubs. Within the Fabaceae family, Deguelia canarensis is part of the subfamily Faboideae, which also includes pea-like plants. Deguelia canarensis is further classified as a member of the tribe Sophoreae, a group of legumes that are native to tropical regions. This classification helps to identify and understand the evolutionary relationships between Deguelia canarensis and other related plant species.

intro: Deguelia canarensis, also known as the Deguelia tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its unique properties and medicinal value. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Deguelia canarensis include deguelin, a lignan with potential antitumor and neuroprotective effects, as well as other alkaloids and flavonoids. Deguelia canarensis is commonly prepared in herbal remedies, such as teas and infusions, which have been traditionally used in African folk medicine to treat various health ailments.
